report_name: US Sanctions VPN Provider 1VPNS for Enabling Ransomware Gangs

threat_actors.name: Dmytro Rashevskyi
threat_actors.description: Ukrainian national and administrator of First VPN Service (1VPNS), sanctioned by the U.S. Treasury for providing infrastructure to ransomware groups.

threat_actors.name: Yegeniy Vladimirovich Silayev
threat_actors.description: Belarusian national sanctioned by the U.S. Treasury for selling 'cryptors', which are services designed to disguise malware and help it evade antivirus detection.

threat_actors.name: Ransomware Groups
threat_actors.description: Various ransomware groups that utilized the services of 1VPNS and Yegeniy Silayev to conduct attacks against U.S. critical infrastructure.

attack_patterns.name: Obfuscated Files or Information
attack_patterns.description: Yegeniy Silayev sold 'cryptors', which are services that disguise malware to evade antivirus detection, a form of obfuscation.

attack_patterns.name: Proxy
attack_patterns.description: First VPN Service (1VPNS) provided anonymous VPN infrastructure that allowed ransomware groups to hide their activities and location.

malware.name: Cryptor Service
malware.description: A malware obfuscation service sold by Yegeniy Silayev to disguise malware payloads and evade antivirus detection.

tools.name: First VPN Service (1VPNS)
tools.description: A VPN provider sanctioned for knowingly providing 'bulletproof' anonymous infrastructure to ransomware groups to conceal their malicious activities.

identities.name: U.S. Department of the Treasury
identities.description: The U.S. government body, through its Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC), that designated and sanctioned 1VPNS and its associates.

identities.name: Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC)
identities.description: The agency within the U.S. Treasury Department that administered the sanctions against 1VPNS.

identities.name: Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI)
identities.description: Partnered with the U.S. Treasury and UK in the action against 1VPNS, including an infrastructure takedown in May 2026.

identities.name: United Kingdom Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office
identities.description: The UK government body that coordinated with the U.S. on the sanctions against 1VPNS.

identities.name: U.S. Critical Infrastructure
identities.description: Victim class targeted by ransomware groups who used the 1VPNS service. Specific sectors mentioned include hospitals and financial services.

infrastructure.name: 1VPNS Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: The VPN infrastructure provided by 1VPNS that was advertised as non-cooperative with law enforcement and used by ransomware groups to hide their operations. Its website and infrastructure were dismantled in May 2026.

indicators.name: Connection to Known Bulletproof VPN Services
indicators.pattern:
title: Connection to Known Bulletproof VPN Services
id: 2b1c8e4a-9f0d-4c6a-8b1e-3d5f7a9b0c2d
status: experimental
description: Detects network connections to IP addresses associated with known bulletproof VPN or hosting services, which are often used by threat actors to anonymize their infrastructure. This rule requires a threat intelligence feed of known bulletproof IPs.
references:
  - https://home.treasury.gov/news/press-releases/sb0559
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-14
tags:
  - attack.t1090
level: medium
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    DestinationIp:
      # This list should be populated by a threat intelligence feed of known bulletproof hosting/VPN IPs.
      - '1.2.3.4' # Placeholder
    Initiated: true
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects network traffic to known 'bulletproof' hosting or VPN services, similar to the service offered by 1VPNS. Detection relies on a threat intelligence feed of associated IP addresses.

indicators.name: 1VPNS Keyword Detection
indicators.pattern:
title: 1VPNS Keyword Detected in Network Traffic
id: 8d3f5c1a-7b9e-4a2d-9c8b-6e4f1a0d3b5c
status: experimental
description: Detects the string '1VPNS' in network logs, which could indicate communication with or discussion of the sanctioned VPN provider.
references:
  - https://home.treasury.gov/news/press-releases/sb0559
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-14
tags:
  - attack.t1090
level: low
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|contains:
      - '1vpns'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the string '1VPNS' in various logs, which could indicate historical or current association with the sanctioned service.

relationships.target_name: United Kingdom Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office

relationships.source_name: Connection to Known Bulletproof VPN Services
